fr.name: Un réSeau de laboratoires ouverts intelligents et innovants dans la région euro-méditerranéenne
fr.description: Sole MED vise à créer un réseau de laboratoires ouverts dans la région euro-méditerranéenne afin de soutenir l'innovation, la croissance des entreprises et la formation des jeunes. Inspiré d'un projet réussi en Italie, il sera adapté à d'autres régions comme l'Espagne, la Grèce et la Bulgarie. Ces espaces collaboratifs aideront les nouvelles entreprises et les petites sociétés grâce à des formations, des outils numériques et des programmes scientifiques et technologiques. Un élément clé du projet est un laboratoire de création de startups axé sur la bioéconomie durable, offrant aux jeunes entrepreneurs une formation pratique en matière de développement commercial, d'innovation et de pratiques respectueuses de l'environnement.
fr.expected_achievements:
The SPECIFIC OBJECTIVE of WP1 is to develop a comprehensive database on active collaborative spaces and bio-economy start-ups across six MED countries. This objective includes conducting an in-depth baseline study on both ecosystems, providing detailed insights into the current state of open innovation labs and the start-up environment in the bio-economy sector. The gathered data will serve as a crucial foundation for the project’s outputs and results, offering valuable information to all targeted groups. By mapping and analyzing existing resources and gaps, WP1 aims to enhance the understanding of the regional innovation landscape, support strategic planning, and foster more effective collaboration and knowledge transfer within the MED area.
The SPECIFIC OBJECTIVE of WP2 is to transfer and upscale the Emilia Romagna Open Labs model to a transnational scale across the EU-MED territories. This will involve adapting the existing business creation and acceleration activities to meet the specific needs and contexts of the MED region. WP2 aims to design and customize this proven model to foster innovation, entrepreneurship, and collaboration in the bio-economy sector within the MED area. By leveraging the successful practices and methodologies of the Emilia Romagna Open Labs, WP2 seeks to create a cohesive network of Open Labs that can support start-ups, enhance regional synergies, and contribute to sustainable economic growth and competitiveness in the bio-economy across the MED region.
The SPECIFIC OBJECTIVE OF WP3 is to tailor and implement the successful “Start-up Creation Lab” model from Emilia Romagna Open Labs to the MED region, specifically targeting young people aged 18 to 30. This will involve designing a comprehensive, region-specific framework to foster bio-economy start-up creation, addressing local needs and opportunities. WP3 will provide targeted training, mentorship, and resources to empower young entrepreneurs, stimulate innovation, and enhance employability. By creating a robust support network, WP3 aims to drive sustainable economic growth, promote environmental sustainability, and contribute to the resilience and inclusiveness of the MED area.
The SPECIFIC OBJECTIVE of WP4 is to implement the “Bio Economy Startup Creation Lab,” targeting youths aged 18 to 30. This initiative aims to foster entrepreneurial skills and stimulate the creation of innovative ventures within the bio-economy sector. WP4 will establish practical, hands-on labs across the MED region, providing young entrepreneurs with comprehensive support, including targeted training, mentorship, and access to essential resources. By creating an environment that encourages collaboration and experimentation, WP4 seeks to empower young people, enhance their employability, and drive the development of sustainable bio-economy enterprises. This effort is designed to contribute to the economic growth and environmental sustainability of the MED area, supporting the broader goals of regional development and innovation.

en.name: Supporting a smarter & innovative Open Lab network in Euro-MED area
en.description: Sole MED aims to create a network of Open Labs across the Euro-Mediterranean region to support innovation, business growth, and training for young people. Inspired by a successful project in Italy, it will be adapted to other areas like Spain, Greece, and Bulgaria. These collaborative spaces will help new businesses and small companies through training, digital tools, and science and technology programs. A key part of the project is a Startup Creation Lab focused on the sustainable bioeconomy, offering young entrepreneurs hands-on training in business development, innovation, and eco-friendly practices.

The specific objective of WP1 is to develop a comprehensive database on active collaborative spaces and bio-economy start-ups across six Euro-MED countries. This objective includes conducting an in-depth baseline study on both ecosystems, providing detailed insights into the current state of open innovation labs and the start-up environment in the bio-economy sector. By mapping and analyzing existing resources and gaps, WP1 aims to enhance the understanding of the regional innovation landscape, support strategic planning, and foster more effective collaboration and knowledge transfer within the Euro-MED area.
The specific objective of WP2 is to transfer and upscale the Emilia-Romagna Open Labs model to a transnational scale across the Euro-MED territories. This will involve adapting the existing business creation and acceleration activities to meet the specific needs and contexts of the Euro-MED region. By leveraging the successful practices and methodologies of the Emilia Romagna Open Labs, WP2 seeks to create a cohesive network of Open Labs that can support start-ups, enhance regional synergies, and contribute to sustainable economic growth and competitiveness in the bio-economy across the Euro-MED region.
The specific objective of WP3 is to tailor and implement the successful “Start-up Creation Lab” model from Emilia-Romagna Open Labs to the MED region, specifically targeting young people aged 18 to 30. This will involve designing a comprehensive, region-specific framework to foster bio-economy start-up creation, addressing local needs and opportunities. WP3 will provide targeted training, mentorship, and resources to empower young entrepreneurs, stimulate innovation, and enhance employability. By creating a robust support network, WP3 aims to drive sustainable economic growth, promote environmental sustainability, and contribute to the resilience and inclusiveness of the MED area.

The specific objective of WP4 is to implement the “Bio-economy startup Creation Lab,” targeting youths aged 18 to 30. This initiative aims to foster entrepreneurial skills and stimulate the creation of innovative ventures within the bio-economy sector. WP4 will establish practical, hands-on labs across the Euro-MED region, providing young entrepreneurs with comprehensive support, including targeted training, mentorship, and access to essential resources.
