en.name: Adapting IUCN Green List Standard to the Mediterranean Marine Protected Areas
en.description:
The “GreenList4MMPAs” Project aims to adapt, for the first time, the IUCN Green List Standard (GL) to the Mediterranean marine context. The GL is the first global and only universal Standard of best practice for area-based conservation. It aims to provide an international benchmark for quality that motivates improved effectiveness and achievement of conservation objectives. GreenList4MMPAs will develop specific indicators and methods to assess marine protected areas (MPAs), providing an actionable and useful tool for MPA managers and practitioners. The common challenge that GreenList4MMPAs will tackle is the need for Mediterranean MPAs to enhance their capacities in preserving marine biodiversity and ecosystems in order to mitigate the overall degradation of the Mediterranean marine environment (fully in line with the Programme S.O. 2.7). GreenList4MMPAs will support Mediterranean MPAs in achieving their conservation and social objectives through a reliable system for the assessment of the effectiveness, based on a tool that will ensure MPA effective management. The main project’s outputs will be the Mediterranean MPAs-tailored IUCN Green List Set of Indicators and the establishment of the MED MPAs EAGLs (a committee of experts trained by IUCN that examine protected areas’ applications for the Green List Standard). These outputs will largely benefit local public authorities such as MPAs’ management bodies, but also national public authorities (e.g. Ministries and technical agencies) and IUCN, which represents globally the largest international organisation about nature conservation.
The “GreenList4MMPAs” Project will adopt an approach based on the use of a large number of case studies in seven EU countries, two IPA countries, and six Third Med countries (thanks to an additional funding by IUCN), in order to represent all the existing seven Ecoregions in the Mediterranean Sea. In this perspective only a true transnational approach can allow project’s goals to be met.
en.expected_achievements:
To adapt the IUCN Green List Standard and to develop Specific indicators for Mediterranean MPAs and other typologies of area-based marine environment protection
Establish the governance structure enabling Mediterranean MPAs to monitor and improve their management effectiveness by applying the IUCN Green list standard.
en.actual_achievements:
GreenList4MMPAs partnership started to meet regularly (mainly online) since the announcement of the Programme approval “under conditions”, to resolve conditions and finalize the contracting procedure. This took quite a long time, also because in February JS communicated the need to have a new PP from an additional eligible country, since the withdrawn of the Albanian PP reduced the total number of participating countries. The new PP (Cote Bleu MPA) was formally taken on board in early March, while the kick-off meeting (with PSC establishment) was held online on January the 26th.
Concerning project implementation, first activity, was the drafting of the Project's activities workplan (Del. 1.1.1). The following activity was the “Review of existing national and international standards to assess MPA effectiveness” (Act. 1.2). Thanks to the results of Act. 1.2, Act. 1.3 “Development of a Mediterranean MPAs-tailored IUCN Green List provisional set of Specific Indicators based on reviewed information” has started.
To harmonize knowledge of all the PPs about the starting reference of the project, a specific training on IUCN Green List Standard was carried out during the presential 2nd PSC project meeting in Rome (9-10 April 2024). 
Act. 2.2 “Drafting of Med MPA EAGLs’ Terms of Reference” (ToRs) has been finalized, with the IUCN Headquarter agreement too. Publication of an open Call for experts (based on the approved ToRs) is expected in October.
Consequently, Act. 2.3 “Selection of experts and establishment of Med MPA EAGLs committee” needs to be moved to the 2nd semester and will be finalised by November 2024. 
LP and PP2 attended the event organized by the Dialogue4Nature governance project (June 27th 2024). 
Internal communication is carried out mainly through e-mails and monthly online meetings. The primary external communications tool implemented is the Project’s website, finalised and published in early June 2024. 
PP2 has developed a communications strategy for the project which, after inputs and approval from the rest of the partners, identified key communications objectives, target audiences, platforms and tools to guide the project´s external communications.

The project is running smoothly. LP assured permanent monitoring activity on the overall project implementation through a stable (monthly) exchange of info among PPs and a regular decision making process (2 Project steering
Committe meetings wereheld). CO2 related data have been input by each PP on the Programme Calculator. 
Concerning WP1, all the steps (analysis of similar existing standards, interviews to MPAs already labelled by Green
List, exploitation of results from similar projects - Act. 1.2 and partially 1.3) have been fulfilled and have allowed to prepare a first draft of the Mediterranean MPAs-tailored IUCN Green List provisional set of Specific Indicators (called also "Guidance Notes") that constitue the deliverable 1.3.1 and have been shared with the project partnership in the final days of the 2nd semester.
Concerning WP2, ToRs and operational rules for the selection and functioning of the "Marine Mediterranean Expert Group for the Green List (Med-EAGL)" have been drafted and adopted by the whole partnership, thus allowing the publication of the "Call for expressions of interest to join the Med-EAGL" that opened on the 14th of November and closed the 11th of December. Candidatures evaluation process, involving also IUCN Headquarters and the IUCN World Commission on Protected Areas (WCPA), was finalised in February 2025.
Concerning the coordination with the "horizontal projects", LP attended the capitalization event organized by the Italian National Contact Point in Florence (November 21st), and the public event organized in Malaga on November 28th and 29 by the mission's governance project.
Finally, as far as communication is concerned, project's section in the Programme website was finalized and the first draft of the project introduction video was realized.
During the IUCN Regional Conservation Forum held in Bruges, where IUCN MED presented GL4MMPAs project, the
Albanian agency of Protection of Environment (NAPA) expressed its interest in joining the project as Associate partner, in order to be informed of the development of the project and contribute to the last phase of the project.
During the third semester, the LP maintained permanent monitoring of the overall project implementation through regular (at least monthly) exchanges of information with PPs and a structured decision-making process. Three Project Steering Committee (PSC) meetings were held: two online (3 February and 13 March) and one in-person (26 June, Carry-le-Rouet, Marseille, hosted by PP6 PMCB with support from PP5 IUCN France).
In its 3rd semester of implementation, the project entered in its crucial phase, i.e. to assess the applicability of the first version of the Mediterranean MPAs-tailored IUCN Green List provisional set of specific indictors (i.e. guidance and notes for applying the Generic Indicators). 
Under LP coordination, the partnership revised Deliverable 1.3.1 (issued in Semester 2) to serve as the reference document for Activity 1.4 “Assessment of applicability of the Mediterranean MPAs-tailored IUCN Green List set of indicators.” The assessment was carried out through an online questionnaire to evaluate respondents’ views on the applicability and clarity of the indicators and related guidance. It was distributed to over 100 Mediterranean stakeholders (MPA managers, Natura 2000 authorities, scientists, practitioners, public bodies and NGOs) from seven EU countries (Spain, France, Italy, Slovenia, Croatia, Greece, Cyprus), two IPA countries (Albania, Montenegro) and MPCs (Turkey, Tunisia, Algeria, Egypt, Lebanon, Libya), thanks to additional IUCN Med resources. Each PP translated the 54 questions into its national language to facilitate participation. IUCN Med also produced two explanatory videos, and SZN with IUCN Med organised two webinars.
In total 45 completed questionnaires were received from all nine EU and IPA countries and from two of the six MPCs, representing a significant sample of the Mediterranean MPA community. Full details are in D1.4.1 “MPAs’ assessment of applicability of the Med MPAs-tailored IUCN Green List set of indicators.”

The LP processed the quantitative data, and in order to manage the big bulk of qualitative feedback (open-end comments) from the questionnaire, 3 internal (i.e. with PPs representatives) working groups were created – during the PSC held in June – for the processing of such inputs and for the drafting of the new version of the “Mediterranean MPAs tailored IUCN Green List provisional set of indicators, with adapted guidance and notes”, to be presented in the forthcoming National Focus Group meetings (Act. 1.5) in order to be furtherly improved.

Regarding Activity 1.5 “National Focus Group meetings” with MPA managers on survey results, the six meetings are scheduled as follows: Spain – early October; France – end of October; Italy – early October; Croatia (with Slovenian and Montenegrin participants) – 16 September; Albania – end of October; Greece – early October. Due to lack of additional resources, no meetings can be organised in Turkey and Tunisia.

Under WP2, the recruitment of the Med MPA EAGL Committee (Activity 2.3), coordinated by IUCN Med, concluded with the appointment of 11 experts covering marine research, MPA management/governance and community engagement. Formally established on 12 March, the committee includes members from four EU countries (Spain, France, Italy, Croatia), one IPA country (Albania), two MPCs (Algeria, Tunisia) and one expert based in the UK. IUCN Med organised a two-day in-person training workshop in Málaga on 12–13 May (Activity 2.4) attended by all members. The training covered the Green List Standard, experts’ roles and a review of the Mediterranean MPAs-tailored Green List guidance (D1.3.1), thus initiating Activity 2.5 “Contribution of Med MPA EAGLs on IUCN Green List adaptation to Med MPAs.” Members were also invited to complete the Activity 1.4 questionnaire (four responses to date) and will take part in Focus Group meetings in their own countries. During the same days, the committee held its first meeting, defined its working methods and elected a chair and co-chair (see D2.4.1 “Training report”).
On communication, coordinated by IUCN Med, the partnership continued to update the project website, share milestones on social media and attend major events such as the “One Ocean Science Congress” (Nice, 4–6 June), the “UN Ocean Conference” (Nice, 9–13 June) and the “High Ambition Coalition on Nature and People” meeting (Nice, 9 June). An introductory video on the main project goals and expected outcomes was produced and subtitled in the project’s six languages (available on YouTube).
Finally, thanks mainly to SZN and IUCN Med, the project continues to coordinate with the Natural Heritage Mission Governance projects, participating in communication meetings, peer-review processes and thematic clusters. IUCN Med also drafted content for the Mission’s “Solve the Challenge” campaign on environmental governance.

fr.description:
GreenList4MMPAs vise à adapter, pour la première fois, le Standard de la Liste Verte (GL) de l'UICN au contexte marin méditerranéen. GL est la première norme mondiale et universelle de meilleures pratiques pour la conservation des zones naturelles. Il vise à fournir une référence internationale de qualité qui motive l'amélioration de l'efficacité et la réalisation des objectifs de conservation. Il développe des indicateurs et des méthodes spécifiques pour évaluer les aires marines protégées (AMP), fournissant un outil exploitable et utile pour les gestionnaires et les praticiens. Le défi commun que GreenList4MMPAs va aborder est la nécessité pour les AMP Med de renforcer leurs capacités à préserver la biodiversité et les écosystèmes marins afin d'atténuer la dégradation globale de l'environnement marin méditerranéen (entièrement conforme au S.O. 2.7). Le projet soutiendra les AMP Med dans la réalisation de leurs objectifs de conservation et sociaux grâce à un système fiable d'évaluation de l'efficacité, basé sur un outil qui assurera une gestion efficace. Les principaux résultats du projet seront l'ensemble d'indicateurs de la GL adaptés aux AMP Med et l'établissement de “AMP MED EAGL” (un comité d'experts formés par l'UICN qui examine les candidatures des AMP pour le Standard GL). Ces résultats bénéficieront aux autorités publiques locales telles que les organes de gestion des AMP, mais aussi aux autorités publiques nationales (ministères et agences techniques) et à l'UICN, qui représente la plus grande organisation internationale de conservation de la nature.
Le projet « GreenList4MMPAs » adoptera une approche basée sur l'utilisation d'un grand nombre d'études de cas dans 7 pays de l'UE, 2 pays IAP et 6 Pays Tiers (grâce à un financement supplémentaire de l'UICN), afin de représenter tous les 7 éco-régions existantes en mer Méditerranée. Dans cette perspective, seule une véritable approche transnationale peut permettre d'atteindre les objectifs du projet.

fr.name: INnovative FInancing solutions for climate planning of REsilient and carbon neutral living areas
fr.description:
La Méditerranée est à l'avant-garde de la guerre contre le changement climatique, ce qui exige des territoires qu'ils agissent rapidement et coopèrent pour prévenir d'autres implications environnementales, sociales et économiques négatives. INFIRE aborde deux aspects clés de la lenteur de la transition vers la neutralité climatique et l'adaptation au climat dans la région Euro-MED: les faibles capacités des autorités publiques pour le développement et l'intégration des documents de planification stratégique et l'incapacité à mobiliser les capitaux publics et privés pour les projets d'investissement nécessaires pour atteindre les objectifs UE pour 2030. 
INFIRE rassemble des partenaires très expérimentés qui coopèrent pour établir une structure de soutien durable afin de renforcer les capacités des autorités publiques pour développer et mettre en œuvre des solutions et des instruments politiques holistiques d'adaptation au changement climatique et de neutralité carbone (CACN). INFIRE fournit des solutions pratiques et développées conjointement:
- Boîte à outils d'évaluation du financement vert pour déclencher le financement de projets CACN et de stratégies à long terme.
- Documents de planification stratégique améliorés et alignés et programmes de financement public pour soutenir la transition.
- Programme de formation intensif et sur mesure pour les autorités publiques de la région Euro-MED.
- Engagement des citoyens par le biais d'un processus de co-création et de modèles de crowdfunding civique.
- Le pilotage de 8 solutions CACN concrètes et innovantes pour réplication et une mise à l'échelle..
L'originalité du projet INFIRE réside dans une approche holistique ascendante qui s'adresse à la fois à l'offre (les autorités publiques en tant que développeurs de projets) et à la demande (les institutions financières et les citoyens en tant qu'investisseurs) en comprenant leurs moteurs et leurs lacunes et en comblant le fossé par une coopération intensive.
fr.expected_achievements:
To identify barriers and provide concrete solutions for economic assessment of public projects and enhancement of financial policies aimed at climate adaptation and carbon neutrality for greener Euro-MED living areas
To increase capacity of local and regional public authorities for effective planning and financing of climate adaptation and carbon neutrality solutions for resilient and greener Euro-MED living areas
To build capacity of public authorities in implementing and monitoring climate adaptation and carbon neutrality solutions for greener Euro-MED living areas and test solutions directly benfitting citizens
Prepare and trigger the transfer and uptake of INFIRE knowledge and solutions and contribute to the Euro-MED Mission Green Living Areas and further Missions of the Euro-MED programme

en.name: INnovative FInancing solutions for climate planning of REsilient and carbon neutral living areas
en.description:
The Mediterranean is at the forefront of the fight against climate change, requiring cities and regions to act quickly and cooperate to prevent further negative environmental, social, and economic implications. INFIRE addresses two key aspects of the slow transition towards climate neutrality and climate adaptation in the Euro-MED region: low capacities of public authorities for the development and integration of strategic planning documents and the inability to properly evaluate and mobilize public and private capital for large-scale investment projects needed for reaching EU’s 2030 targets. 

INFIRE gathers experienced partners with highly complementary skills that cooperate to establish a long-lasting support structure to build capacities of public authorities in the involved regions for developing, implementing, and monitoring holistic climate adaptation and carbon neutrality (CACN) solutions and policy instruments. INFIRE provides jointly developed and practical solutions in form of:
-	Green Finance Evaluation Toolkit for triggering financing of CACN projects and long-term strategies with innovative financing schemes
-	Enhanced and aligned strategic planning documents and public financing programmes to support the transition to resilience and carbon neutrality
-	Tailor-made curriculum and intensive capacity-building programme for public authorities of the Euro-MED region
-	Citizens’ engagement through co-creation process and civic-crowdfunding models, promoting behavioral change
-	Piloting 8 concrete and innovative CACN solutions for future replication and upscaling.

The originality of INFIRE project arises from a holistic bottom-up approach that addresses both the supply (public authorities as project developers) and demand side (financial institutions and citizens as investors) by understanding their drivers and shortcomings and bridging the gap through intensive cooperation with both sides and promotion of best available practices.
en.expected_achievements:
To identify barriers and provide concrete solutions for economic assessment of public projects and enhancement of financial policies aimed at climate adaptation and carbon neutrality for greener Euro-MED living areas
To increase capacity of local and regional public authorities for effective planning and financing of climate adaptation and carbon neutrality solutions for resilient and greener Euro-MED living areas
To build capacity of public authorities in implementing and monitoring climate adaptation and carbon neutrality solutions for greener Euro-MED living areas and test solutions directly benfitting citizens
Prepare and trigger the transfer and uptake of INFIRE knowledge and solutions and contribute to the Euro-MED Mission Green Living Areas and further Missions of the Euro-MED programme
en.actual_achievements:
During the first reporting period, the INFIRE project made substantial progress in establishing its administrative framework and initiating key activities, despite facing some delays. This phase focused on building a solid foundation for future work, emphasizing partnerships, project management, and initial research efforts.
The administrative setup included the  finalization of  the Partnership Agreement and Subsidy Contract, and formalizing the project's structure. Active participation in Steering Committee meetings ensured coordinated action across the work packages. Essential tools and templates for financial framework assessments and gap analyses have been under preparion setting the stage for more detailed assessments in the next period.
Regarding activity progress, the work on innovative green financing focused on the preparation of critical templates for the  assessment and the initiation of a gap analysis of financing models, providing a foundation for exploring financing mechanisms across regions. Methodologies and tools, such as questionnaires, were developed to guide the integration and improvement of policy instruments, ensuring alignment between different work packages. Work also began on reviewing regional frameworks for climate change adaptation and financing opportunities, adapting the project to evolving local needs.
Communication and engagement efforts such as  the project’s website, branding materials, and social media presence were created to raise awareness, and the drafting of a Communication Strategy was initiated. Efforts were also made to engage associated partners, forming working groups with local municipalities to ensure  the widespread dissemination of project objectives through various channels.
Challenges during this period included delays in finalizing the Subsidy Contract, which affected the start of activities across work packages, leading to the postponement of certain deliverables. Additionally, there were difficulties in appointing external consultants for pilot activities, but despite these obstacles, core activities were launched with minimal delay.Transnational cooperation has been a valuable asset to the project, allowing partners to leverage expertise from different regions, particularly in green financing, climate resilience, and policy integration. 
In summary, the first reporting period primarily focused on administrative setup and managing initial delays. Nevertheless, the project managed to prepare assessments, forming regional partnerships, and launching communication efforts, setting the stage for more impactful outputs in the next phase.

Second period marked substantial progress despite an important transition in leadership, with the Region of Peloponnese (REGPEL) transferring the Lead Partner role to the North-West Croatia Regional Energy and Climate Agency (REGEA). This transition was discussed, voted on, and officially adopted through multiple SC meetings, ensuring a smooth and structured shift. REGEA established structured coordination mechanisms, including monthly online and biannual in-person meetings, to strengthen leadership, optimize resource allocation, and prevent project delays. Importantly, these changes did not affect non-management project activities, with REGPEL retaining its responsibilities apart from coordination.

A key milestone was the in-person SC meeting in Zagreb in October 2024, where the modification of the Lead Partner role was confirmed within the Partnership. The subsequent online SC meeting in December 2024 further solidified coordination mechanisms and partner engagement.

In WP1, INFIRE partners completed an assessment report analysing financing frameworks across the Euro-MED regions and a gap analysis on innovative green finance mechanisms and investment procedures. Further advancements were made with developing key financial tools, such as the socio-economic evaluation methodology for CACN projects, the INFIRE Financial and Economic Evaluation Tool, and a Decision-Making Cost Comparator. These tools equip public authorities with structured guidance for assessing investment options and optimising strategies.

Stakeholder engagement has also been a major success. A roadmap for engaging financial institutions, policymakers, and project ambassadors was completed. Related to stakeholder engagement, REGEA organised the first National Roundtable session, which was held in December 2024 in Karlovac, Croatia. Other partners have begun planning similar sessions in their regions.

Under WP2, partners defined the methodology for integrating and improving policy instruments for resilient and carbon-neutral living areas. Partners participated in a joint analysis of selected policy instruments, identifying integration opportunities and evaluating local planning capacities. This process included data collection, SWOT and PESTLE analyses, analyses of the city CACN-related policy instruments and assessment of the city capacity and knowledge gaps.

WP3 focused on preparing for the testing and demonstration of CACN solutions. Achievements in this WP include developing a methodology for assessing pilot project performance, defining a structured approach for pilot monitoring, and establishing key performance indicators (KPIs) relevant to CACN solutions. 

A significant component of WP3 has been the launch of territorial working groups to engage citizens and stakeholders. DVISION delivered the INFIRE Communication Strategy along with promotional materials such as the INFIRE leaflet, pilot flyers, a promotional video, newsletters, brochures, posters, and a roll-up banner. These materials aim to enhance public awareness and participation in the project’s climate adaptation initiatives.

In WP4, INFIRE strengthened its role within the broader Euro-MED mission for Green Living Areas. PP’s participated in an online session of the C4LA Thematic Working Group on Financing the Green Transition, held on December 12, 2024. During this session, project representatives exchanged insights with other initiatives, identified opportunities for collaboration, and outlined joint activities for 2025. Additionally, all PP’s collected carbon footprint data for the second period, contributing to a more comprehensive evaluation of the project’s environmental impact.

Throughout the second implementation period, INFIRE has demonstrated the significant added value of transnational cooperation in addressing climate adaptation and carbon neutrality challenges. By uniting diverse partners with complementary expertise, the project has successfully developed practical financial tools, started working on strengthening policy frameworks, and initiated meaningful stakeholder engagement processes. As the project progresses, the focus will be on scaling up pilot initiatives, refining methodologies based on real-world testing, and ensuring the long-term sustainability of CACN solutions through effective policy integration and financing mechanisms.

en.name: Towards Climate Resilient Mediterranean Cities
en.description:
Urban areas are known to be among the main contributors to climate change worldwide. At the same time, they are especially at risk from the impacts of climate change. There is a growing recognition that climate change is here for the long term, and that actions is needed, not just mitigation, but also to adapt to the impacts that we can expect to see now and in the future. Consequently, innovative responses for raising the resilience and adaptation of Mediterranean cities are needed. Capitalising and combining the results of previous and ongoing projects (CESBA MED Sustainable MED Cities, Sustainable MED Cities, ARTACLIM and Habit.A), ReMED will develop an innovative set of affordable tools, along with an overarching decision support framework, to help cities in:
- assessing and understanding the level of climate risk at urban and building scale
- designing optimal climate adaptation measures in relation to local conditions
- implementing climate adaptation measures through the most suitable policy instruments
- monitoring and evaluating the results of adaptation measures over time.
ReMED will support a Multi-Level Governance (MLG) in climate adaptation, as recommended by the UN COP26.
ReMED methods, tools and decision support framework will be organised in a friendly web-based tool, the ReMED Platform. ReMED will deliver capacity building measures towards public authorities in the use of the ReMED Platform to facilitate its practical use and uptake. The focus of the capacity building process will be the test of the ReMED Platform to support the implementation of adaptation measures through policy instruments. The main output of the project will be an overall raised capacity of public authorities in deploying effective measures to improve the resilience of cities.
en.expected_achievements:
Set up of the ReMED Platform, and integrated set of tools and methodologies to guide cities in the definition and implementation of optimal adaptation measures.
Test of the ReMED Platform on 5 pilot case studies to facilitate its uptake and use by public and local-regional authorities in synergy with climate policy instruments. The test will allow to identify possible improvements of the ReMED Platform.  
This WP will be focused on integrating the ReMED Platform into different policy instruments to maximize their effectiveness and impact as tools for the implementation of optimal resilience and adaptation measures.
en.actual_achievements:
The first period of the ReMED project was an eventful period. The project started off with the kick-off meeting held in Malta and organized by the Lead Partners, University of Malta. This was also the consortium first Steering Committee meeting. During the meeting it was communicated to all the partners that one of the Greek partners decided not to proceed with the project and thus a new Greek Partner had to be found. It was a long process to find and change the partner and get all the paperwork done so that the contract agreement and subsidiary agreement could be concluded and signed. 

During this period Green Living also launched their kick-off meeting in Torino in which representatives of all the projected met to discuss the Green living theme and how we can collaborate between projects. 

In this period the first unofficial deliverable was tackled, which is ‘Mapping existing methodologies and indicators for climate change urban risk assessment’. This output was led by the National Observatory of Athens (NOA) in which all partners contributed to this document. This document was submitted on Basecamp by NOA. We also worked on the setting up the Local Project Committee in which ReMED project was introduced. A document, outlining the meeting was also submitted on Basecamp. This output was led by iiSBE Italia. 

In the Communication department, a lot of work was also done. The Communication Strategy was drawn up by University of Malta, reviewed by all partners and then submitted on Basecamp. Other communication items which were set up during this first period were the project website, social media pages on Facebook and LinkedIn, project poster and project document templates for our outputs and presentations. 
The Project Carbon Footprint monitoring was also addressed based on the methodology and tool provided by the Interreg Euro Med Programme I which all Project Partners calculated the carbon footprint generated by the project to date. This is led by NOA. 

The project is progressing at a good pace and mostly on schedule as planned. In this period, we had one official deliverable, to be completed: A1.2 - ReMED Assessment Systems. This was delivered at the end of period 2 successfully.  The development of the ReMED Assessment System is an important milestone in the ReMED project, as it lays the foundations and mechanism for the ReMED assessment tool.  

Another two unofficial deliverables, A1.3 and A1.4. are being completed:
A1.3 was targeted to be complete by the end of Period 2. These two activities deal with the Decision Support Framework and tackle the ReMED Platform. Good progress was registered in these activities. The ReMED team realised that these deliverables would not be complete by the end of Period 2 due the vast amount of work the activity and official deliverable A1.2 entailed.  For this reason, the target date for the finalisation of the two unofficial deliverables A1.3 and A1.4 was shifted to the beginning of Period 3. This gave the opportunity to have a better quality output for these activities. They are currently on schedule and should be finalised in the first few months of Period 3 as planned.
During period 3 we had one official deliverable D1.7.1 - The ReMED video which was submitted in January and two unofficial deliverables WD2.1.1 - Adaptation of the ReMED system and WD 2.3.1 - Training Protocol; as well as A1.3 - Decision support framework which was shifted from period 2 to period 3. All deliverables were submitted as planned. We also had a Partner meeting in Athens / Fyli Municipality in Greece, and discussions with different partners to make sure the project was on track. Online meetings in between the partner physical meetigns were also organised to ensure project deliverables are on track. The ReMEDTraining system framework was consolidated during the Project meeting organised and hosted in Athens and Fyli Municpality; the material will be prepared during period 4. There was a push from the Lead Partners to ensure that all Municaplity partners finalsie the preparation and issue of the tenders to engage the external experts as envisaged in the project proposal. This is an important step to commence the testing phase with the municipalities partners in the project. Communication of the project continued with different partners ensuring dissemination in the resepctive countries and regions.

fr.name: Vers des villes méditerranéennes résilientes au changement climatique
fr.description:
Les zones urbaines sont connues pour être parmi les principaux contributeurs au changement climatique dans le monde. Dans le même temps, ils sont particulièrement menacés par les impacts du changement climatique. Il est de plus en plus reconnu que le changement climatique est là pour le long terme et que des actions sont nécessaires, non seulement pour l'atténuation, mais aussi pour s'adapter aux impacts que nous pouvons nous attendre à voir maintenant et à l'avenir. Par conséquent, des réponses innovantes pour accroître la résilience et l'adaptation des villes méditerranéennes sont nécessaires. Capitalisant les résultats des projets précédents (CESBA MED, ARTACLIM et Habit.A), ReMED développera des outils abordables, ainsi qu'un cadre global d'aide à la décision, pour aider les villes à :
- évaluer et comprendre le niveau de risque climatique à différentes échelles spatiales
- concevoir des mesures optimales d'adaptation au climat en fonction des contextes locaux
- mettre en œuvre les mesures d'adaptation au climat par le biais d'instruments politiques à fort impact
- suivre et évaluer les résultats des mesures d'adaptation dans le temps.
ReMED soutiendra une gouvernance multi-niveaux (MLG) dans l'adaptation au climat, comme recommandé par la COP26.
Les méthodes, les outils et le cadre d'aide à la décision ReMED seront organisés dans une application Web conviviale, la plateforme ReMED. ReMED fournira des mesures de renforcement des capacités aux autorités publiques dans l'utilisation de la plateforme ReMED afin de faciliter son utilisation pratique et son adoption. Le processus de renforcement des capacités se concentrera sur le test de la plateforme ReMED pour soutenir la mise en œuvre de mesures d'adaptation à travers un ensemble d'instruments politiques de référence. Le principal résultat du projet sera une capacité globale accrue des autorités publiques à déployer des mesures efficaces pour améliorer la résilience des villes.

fr.name: COoperation and CO-designing PArtnership for CliMAte Neutrality
fr.description:
Les îles méditerranéennes vont entamer leur transition vers la neutralité climatique grâce au projet CO2 Pacman.
Atraver les résultats et les outils des précédents projets (BLUE DEAL, COMPOSE/COMPOSE Plus, FP7 CityZen), CO2 Pacman fournira les connaissances et les outils nécessaires pour les stratégies et de politiques intégrées pour relever le défi de l'atténuation du changement climatique et de l'adaptation à celui-ci ; à cette fin, il engagera les autorités publiques, les citoyens et les parties prenantes dans la co-création de scénarios pour la transition vers des îles climatiquement neutres.
Les activités du projet seront présentées dans 3 îles: Brac (HR), Crète(EL), Elbe (IT).
Le projet développera un cadre coopératif de planification intégrée et fournira une stratégie/un plan d'action conjoint intégrant des politiques et des stratégies d'atténuation du changement climatique et d'adaptation à celui-ci, qui pourront être reproduites dans les îles et les zones côtières. Le nouvel outil d'évaluation et de visualisation du CO2 Pacman sera perfectionné et utilisé pour évaluer les défis actuels (e.g. l'empreinte carbone et ses facteurs), puis pour identifier un ensemble de solutions multisectorielles (dans les domaines de l'énergie, des bâtiments, de la mobilité, de la gestion de l'eau et des déchets, du tourisme, de l'industrie), basées sur des approches écosystémiques, pour quantifier leurs effets en termes d'atténuation et de compensation de l'empreinte carbone pour l'objectif de neutralité climatique dans les îles.
L'approche de coopération et de co-création ciblera les autorités publiques, les entreprises, les groupes d'intérêt et les citoyens pour soutenir l'innovation et le renforcement des capacités.
Le passage à une société climatiquement neutre nécessite une transition culturelle: le projet intègre des informations scientifiques et techniques dans un nouveau système de communication capables d'aider à planifier la transition climatique pour les îles.
fr.expected_achievements:
Co-creation of CO2 Pacman assessment framework, identification of key factors for GHG emissions/reduction and data collection/elaboration for climate neutrality. Development of visualization tools for involvement of PA, citizens of pilot islands
Multi-level involvement of stakeholders, i.e. public authorities, businesses, students and citizens, promoting an inclusive approach for the acceptance, development, co-operation, and co-design of  transition towards climate neutral islands
Development of tailored, knowledge-based roadmaps towards climate neutrality of Mediterranean islands, embedding strategies/policies for climate change mitigation/adaptation, co-designed with PAs, citizens and students using virtual reality and tools

en.name: COoperation and CO-designing PArtnership for CliMAte Neutrality
en.description:
Mediterranean islands will start their transition to climate neutrality thanks to the CO2 Pacman project.
Building on outcomes and tools from the previous projects Interreg Med BLUE DEAL and COMPOSE Plus and the FP7 CityZen, CO2 Pacman will provide knowledge and tools to drive the implementation of integrated strategies and policies for the challenge of climate change mitigation and adaptation; with this purpose it will engage public authorities, citizens and stakeholders in co-creation of scenarios for the transition towards climate neutral islands.
Project activities will be showcased in three target islands: Brac in Croatia, Crete in Greece and Elba in Italy. 
Based on results from testing, the project will develop an integrated cooperative planning framework and deliver a joint strategy/action plan embedding policies and strategies for climate change mitigation and adaptation to be potentially replicated in islands and coastal areas. The new CO2 Pacman assessment and visual tool will be developed to assess current challenges (i.e. Carbon Footprint and its drivers) and then to identify multi-sectoral solutions and to quantify their effects in terms of Carbon Footprint mitigation and compensation to achieve the goal of climate neutrality in the islands. These solutions, including ecosystem based approaches, will concern the sectors of energy, buildings, mobility, water and waste management, tourism, industry, etc.
The cooperation and co-creation approach will target: public authorities (increasing their capacity in effective planning), enterprises, groups of interest and citizens to foster both transnational and local cooperation, support innovation and capacity building.
The shift to a climate neutral society requires a cultural transition: the project integrates scientific and technical information in a new clear and fun communication and social engagement system, composed by new visual tools able to help plan climate neutrality transition roadmaps for islands.
en.expected_achievements:
Co-creation of CO2 Pacman assessment framework, identification of key factors for GHG emissions/reduction and data collection/elaboration for climate neutrality. Development of visualization tools for involvement of PA, citizens of pilot islands
Multi-level involvement of stakeholders, i.e. public authorities, businesses, students and citizens, promoting an inclusive approach for the acceptance, development, co-operation, and co-design of  transition towards climate neutral islands
Development of tailored, knowledge-based roadmaps towards climate neutrality of Mediterranean islands, embedding strategies/policies for climate change mitigation/adaptation, co-designed with PAs, citizens and students using virtual reality and tools
en.actual_achievements:
CO2PACMAN project officially started on November 21st 2023. The Kick-Off Meeting was held on 20-22 March 2024 in Siena (Italy), and established the groundwork for project implementation, with discussions on roles, responsibilities, and timelines. Before that several informal online meetings were organized to prepare activities. The project management structure was presented and has been fully operational since the project kick-off meeting. The Project Management Team has been established by UNISI and includes representatives from all partner organizations, ensuring efficient coordination and communication. As LP, UNISI has continually assured the flow of communication between PPs ensuring the circulation of the relevant information concerning the contents of the project, as well as administrative and financial issues. Since the beginning, all project partners were engaged and actively contributed to the project development, except for RAUSK who withdrew from the Partnership after a few months. The CO2PACMAN Steering Committee decided to replace RAUSK, with all its budget, with the new Bosnian partner CENER21. 
For this reason, the procedure to modify the AF has been finalized in delay by the LP in accordance with the SC members and the JS and the signature of the contract process has been concluded in July 2024.
The LP and the WP leaders coordinated the co-working activities of PPs. Online meetings were held to discuss progress, address issues, and ensure alignment across all work packages. A budget Redistribution among UNIFI and UNISI Partners has been performed and approved by the Steering Committee. UNIFI asked UNISI to take over the coordination role of the WP1. This decision was derived due to the change in composition of the UNIFI team, and the loss of the previous reference person and related expertise. While minor challenges have arisen, they have been effectively managed without significant impact on the timeline or budget. 
Clear instructions and guidelines were sent to PPs to allow PP delegates to provide their contribution, collect coherent information, and assemble final deliverables by WP leaders. 
Partner UNISI worked closely with Partner UNIFI to Activity 1.1 implementation- Preparing Pilot activities: CO2PACMAN tool assessment system. The 1.1 activity has been performed based on remote meetings and several co-working sessions. The deliverable D1.1 has been produced and definitively validated by the quality Board of the CO2PACMAN Project. 
Partner EPLO submitted the first draft of D.2.5- Development of communication and dissemination strategy, contributing to the project's overall understanding of the communication strategy. Managing the project’s dissemination and communication efforts, ensuring that the project’s progress and results were effectively shared with a broader audience. The project website has been also created and populated with news and information. Social Media channels (LinkedIn, Facebook, YouTube, and Instagram) have been activated and populated with information on the project's activities. A Podcast Show was launched and video interviews and video presentations of the partnership were created and produced by the LP and launched in the project social community to communicate about the project.
Activity 1.2- Preparing pilot activities: cooperative planning has been started by Resel-TUC Partner, and will be finalized in the coming months. A longer period was required to achieve a common understanding among project partners and to develop a joint motivation and strategy. For this reason ReSEL-TUC initiated a consultation matrix exercise, to integrate and streamline all views, incentives and expectations. Also, the team put some effort into making links with previous initiatives (i.e. BLUE DEAL, COMPOSE)  to build on experiences, tested methods and, exploring potential synergies  for a more effective implementation. The deliverable D1.2.1 will be soon available in Basecamp.

In general, all the partners provided essential resources and expertise that enabled WP and task Leaders to finalize the specific deliverable of this first reporting period. Procedures for internal communication have been created and agreed upon between all PPs, including the creation of the Communication Team (made by one person per PP), and the shared Google Drive folder as the repository for all the project documents. The foreseen Communication activity for 2024 has been summarized by the CM and shared with all PPs.
During the initial semester, the LP and the Communication manager participated in the Kick-off of the Green Living Areas Community project in Turin (in April 2024). 
Overall, the project is on track to meet its goals, and the consortium is committed to maintaining momentum in the next reporting period. Each partner has made substantial contributions, both independently and in collaboration with others, ensuring that the project remains on track and continues to deliver high-quality outputs.

During this reporting period, key events and activities ensured active participation and coordination across the CO2 PACMAN partnership.The new Partner CENER21 participated to the all the activities of the project. Partners organized and facilitated the Rooting Labs in Brač (Sept 2024, co-organized by SDC and SDEWES) and Crete (Oct 2024, organized by ReSEL-TUC), promoting stakeholder cooperation and fostering community involvement in decarbonization. Additionally, several coordination meetings were held to ensure the smooth progress of project activities.
The finalized Communication and Dissemination Strategy is the primary guide for the project's external communication efforts, ensuring consistency and outreach across all partners. The project website, particularly the News & Events section, was updated in this framework to keep stakeholders informed. Project partners presented CO2 PACMAN in some events to communicate the project's objectives, share insights, and foster new collaborations. In particular, UNISI participated in the 1st Institutional Policy Dialogue 1st Forum EuroMED Italia, organized by the Italian NCP. Besides, delegates of CO2 PACMAN attended some events organized by the GLA Mission. All PPs participated in the Rooting Lab workshops in Brač and Crete, contributing to the project's activities and visibility. 
EPLO supported the project's communication activities, providing input at the GLA Communication Managers meeting and contributing to the overall communication strategy. In addition, the project engages with the public through various initiatives, such as promoting CO2 PACMAN activities through media campaigns, online platforms,  podcasts and video interviews. 
ReSEL-TUC engaged with local and national press, supporting campaigns for Crete's climate neutrality and ensuring widespread outreach across regional communities. SDC and SDWES Center promoted the 1st Rooting Lab across regional and local media. Besides, four new episodes of the CO2 PACMAN podcast shows were recorded and published (created in cooperation with UNISI’s AP URadio), all in English and the local language of the speakers. The topics covered were the role of the Ecodynamics group of the UNISI, the 1st and 2nd Rooting Labs, and the role of the SMEs. GIITT strengthened public engagement by facilitating in-person surveys and interviews of citizens during the labs, thus increasing the local community’s understanding of the project’s goals and fostering awareness among residents.
UNISI and UNIFI has started with the engagement of local stakeholders and logistics for the 3rd Rooting Lab in Elba that is planned for February 2025. UNIFI has also organized a data collection on landscape planning in all three islands.
Specific WP1 technical activities were integral to the project’s implementation, including developing the Cooperative Planning Guidelines (D1.2), the SWOT Analysis and Action Plan for Brač and Crete (D1.1), and preparing detailed CO2 datasets. ReSEL-TUC led Crete's and SDEWES led the Brac’s successful data collection efforts, assembling datasets on demographics, land use, and energy consumption, which will inform future climate neutrality strategies for the island. Additionally, preparations for the CO2 PACMAN tool are underway to support data analysis and collaborative planning efforts. Once finalized, the tool will be pivotal for stakeholder engagement, allowing local authorities to utilize the gathered data in designing and implementing tailored decarbonization strategies.
In WP2, progress was made in Stakeholder Engagement activities, with CENER21 contributing to the development of engagement methodologies, CEEI Valencia leading the preparation for the Open Innovation Collaborative Actions;  
As part of WP2 communication objectives, social media campaigns were successfully launched by GIINT alongside offline activities such as Vox Populi interviews and questionnaires to increase awareness and gather input from local stakeholders. These efforts were complemented by creating engaging content, including event videos and press releases during the rooting Labs, ensuring wide dissemination of the project’s key messages and outcomes.
The project has also made significant strides in participatory planning processes, with partners contributing to developing methodologies and tools that will be crucial for future planning. The use of Virtual Reality led by GEOIMAGING partner and showcased during the 1st and 2nd Rooting Labs were essential for the engagement phase. The engagement of stakeholders, including local citizens and decision-makers, continues to be a cornerstone of the project’s success.
The reporting period saw considerable advancements in developing and disseminating CO2 PACMAN’s objectives and successful collaboration between partners. These efforts continue to lay a solid foundation for the project’s upcoming phases, paving the way for effective decarbonization solutions tailored to Mediterranean islands

The project has made significant progress since its launch, achieving several of its specific objectives and delivered the KEY deliverables D1.3.1 and D1.4. 1. The 1st round of Labs was concluded with the Rooting Lab held in Elba (IT) and Florence (IT) on February 11–13, 2025. A central achievement has been the coordination among partners, enabling the (substantially) timely implementation of key activities and the alignment of efforts towards shared goals. Several online meetings monitored progress and ensured alignment across WPs. Two Steering Committees were organized, complemented by several online meetings to ensure alignment of activities. Partners received guidelines to provide inputs and prepare deliverables under WP leaders’ supervision. An external evaluation report to present the results of the external Quality Assessment was carried out. This evaluation identified main strengths, weaknesses, and critical issues, providing valuable input for steering the next implementation phases.
One of the project results is the implementation of the CO2 PACMAN tool, which has involved also the PPs with the data collection for Brač, Elba and Crete and the subsequent elaboration by UNISI. The tool focuses on GHG emission and landscape data for decarbonization scenarios. 
Social media channels (LinkedIn, Facebook, YouTube, and Instagram) remained active and were regularly updated with project content by EPLO with the collaboration of all PPs. The first newsletter was created and distributed, the video summary of the project realized; 6 new episodes of CO2 PACMAN podcast were realized (in collaboration with all the PPs and uRadio); 6 video interview VOX POPULI have been realized and delivered during Elba Lab by GIINT.
Under WP2 “Multilevel Participation,” substantial effort went into Activity 2.1 ‘Participatory Planning’ and Activity 2.2 ‘Willingness to Change. For the Youth Think Tank activity, that included remote meetings, co-working sessions, and in-person exchanges, PPs UNISI and UNIFI went to I.S.I.S. Foresi hight school in Elba (March 11, 2025). ReSEL-TUC organized a 2-week co-design project to engage university students in shaping climate-neutral scenarios for Crete. 
The Open Innovation Collaborative Action was developed and implemented and finalized with the Valencia Business Forum, organized by CEEI. They published previously identified challenges (55 solutions) on a dedicated platform, (specific developed with EPLO support), engaged numerous stakeholders, and facilitated evaluation through 9 Local Technical Committees, selecting 27 solutions to present at the Forum (25-26 June 2025). 4 of the challengers were attending  the Business Forum in Valencia and interacted with the solvers, 9 solvers present phisically to the event and also online. The expenditures of these attendees have been covered by GEO, CEEI, SDEWES PPs. AskyourcitizenonCaN Survey and questionnaires were conducted on each pilot island (UNIFI on Elba, TUC on Crete, SDEWES and SDC on Brac) and statistical data analysis was started for the realization of a statistical report  elaborated by UNISI. While AskyourcitizenonCaN platform, for the interactive part and disseminating results, are in the implementation phase and is under construction and delivered by PPs GIINT.  
CENER21 drafted a roadmap, timelines, stakeholder tables, and outreach templates, coordinating with UNISI to align engagement strategies, finalized the Stakeholder Engagement Plan for the next "The Island I would like" Labs. In particular for Brač, we have tarted the organization of the second round of Labs preparing the draft agenda for the Lab, and completed the Data Collection and Engagement Methods document.
UNISI, together with CENER21, SDC and SDEWES contributed to preparatory steps for the Brač Lab, including stakeholder outreach, venue planning, and logistical coordination with SDC. All partners provided essential resources and expertise enabling WP and task leaders to finalize deliverables.
PPs UNIFI and GEO provided design samples of energy transition sustainable landscape changes to be developed and experienced via Virtual Reality.
EPLO, coordinated CO2 PACMAN's presence at the Circle the Med Forum, with the LP and SDC. Participation in the GLA events was relevant: in Patras EPLO presented achievements of CO2 PACMAN; CEEI  was attending the meeting in Valencia; UNISI and EPLO were present at the "2nd Gathering GLA mission community" in Barcellona; GEO showcased CO2PACMAN outputs in a workshop delivered under the Alliance4XR project in Portugal, at Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, as well as as during a vocational training of young engineers learning Green skills in Cyprus.
UNIFI participated (showing results from CO2 PACMAN) in the international exhibition "Biennale di Architettura 2025" in Venice; UNISI presented at the International Week on Resilient and Sustainable Cities in Brazil (BR) the main results of the project.

en.name: Increasing capacities of PTO and PTA in the EURO MED area by testing novel solutions to effectively manage public transport’s transition towards energy-efficiency and resource-efficiency
en.description: E-MED tests and validates solutions to increase energy-efficiency and resource-efficiency of public transport systems to reduce public transport's environmental footprint drastically, and to react flexibly to changing energy prices caused by the current energy crisis. This challenge affects in particular local public authorities, public transport infrastructure and energy providers, but also industry suppliers of electric vehicles and service operators. Thus, E-MED solutions are developed to increase energy-efficiency and share of Renewable Energy Systems (RES), (e.g., tools for fleet, network planning, driver support, Artificial intelligence supported maintenance of energy systems), and to increase resource-efficiency and resilience, (e.g., re-use of batteries and washing water recycling systems, maintenance of tires, procurement guidelines) support in the evidence-based planning and introduction of e-bus systems to help objective decision-taking and smart planning, investing and procurement from the start. This way, E-MED solutions support the right design of a Public Transport (PT) system through smart organisation and planning of networks and operations, optimised charging concepts, optimal use of engineering technology, and capacity building around more “human” topics like eco-driving. As key enablers to reduce energy consumption and to increase the lifetime of PT fleet and infrastructure, E-MED solutions with focus on end-user needs provide orientation to both buyers and industry. Based on the testing and validation of E-MED solutions, strategies and action plans are optimised in a participatory co-design approach with stakeholders from the public transport and energy community. E-MED is composed of 10 partners ranging from public transport organisations and authorities to cities, as well as knowledge partners renowned in the transport and energy field. To enlarge this cooperation, associated paratners like UITP, University of Lisbon, and CARNET are strategically involved to maximise communication outreach and knowledge transfer of results.
en.expected_achievements:
Provide ready-for take-up solutions to support 250 PT stakeholders with evidence-based decision-taking to improve the energy-efficiency and resilience in different electric PT subsystems: vehicle, network, energy facility.
 Provide ready-for take-up solutions to reduce the energy consumption of ebuses by means of eco-driving tools and Artificial Intelligence applied to the telemetric data.

Adapted to the needs of 500 PTA and PTO’s, develop solutions with 10 e-bus manufacturers and 10 solution providers to increase resource-efficiency of fleet, infrastructure, services, and operations.
Develop strategies and action plans and a blueprint for coordinated, evidence-based, planning and financing, following an integrated and participatory, to enable 100 PTA and PTO improve their public transport decarbonisation strategies.
en.actual_achievements:
In the first weeks of Period 1, the E-MED partners prepared the Steering Committee and Partnership agreements, and modified the Application Form in Jems as per JS's pre-contracting instructions. These modifications were officially approved on April 17, 2024. The kick-off meeting took place in Barcelona on February 28, 2024, with representatives from each partner.

Two additional General Assembly meetings (April 17 and June 19, 2024) were held to support project implementation, engage partners, and plan future activities.

Partners set up coordination tools for online communication and project management (Basecamp, Google Meet, M. SharePoint) and created a project website and LinkedIn channel using the JS-provided template.

Activities 1.1 and 1.2 of WP1 consisted of:

-	Conceptualizing the toolkit with integrated network-, vehicle-, and charging facility-level capabilities (all partners).
-	Defining ICT platform requirements to integrate Geographic Information Systems with modeling capabilities (CERTH, Theta).
-	Enhancing the vehicle-level planning tool by IDIADA, with AI-powered chat support for electric bus inquiries using IDIADA's data (IDIADA, CERTH, UPC).
-	Conducting a market analysis of charging-facility planning tools and identifying gaps (TUSGSAL, UPC).
-	Defining user requirements and toolkit capabilities (related to WP4).
-	Preparing content for a workshop in Thessaloniki to introduce the WP1 Planning Toolkit.

In WP2, the structure and content for the State of the Art analysis based on Eco-driving were initiated in A2.1 (supervised by UM and MOM). The specific advancements were:
-	Reviewing tools and guidelines for diesel solutions and assessing their adaptation to electric fleets, as well as Eco-driving benefits (Carris, ATB, TUSGSAL, MOM).
-	Identifying solutions for vehicles, drivers, control centers, and technology (automated, semi-automated, training).
-	Reviewing monitoring, implementation, planning, and driver reward schemes, along with regulations and requirements.
-	Developing a questionnaire for the State of the Art analysis at pilot locations (Maribor, Barcelona, Lisbon).
In Activity 2.2, UPC (Leader) and the PTOs of the E-MED project collaborated to analyze operational data from buses captured by each operator's telemetric systems. Sample datasets were obtained from TUSGSAL, Carris and ATB. The partners also undertook the following tasks:
-	Standardizing the datasets: Developed a code to translate raw data from each operator into a harmonized file format for consistency across cities.
-	Conceptualizing outputs: Defined data analytics outputs (powered by AI) in three areas: fleet analysis, driver analysis, and vehicle analysis.
-	Developing algorithms: Coded algorithms in Python for integration into the WP2 Toolkit to perform fleet analysis, focusing on headway and speed variations along routes.
The achievements in WP3 (led by TUSGSAL) aligned with the procurement methodology for fleet and infra. (A3.1) and green and circular procurement for services and operations (A3.2). The specific contributions during Period 1 were:
-	Conducting a benchmark of the current situation through interviews and surveys with PTOs, PTAs, and other partners involved in the project.
-	Defining the requirements for small to medium pilot tests, incorporating learnings and insights from past procurement processes.
-	Developing guidelines for green and circular procurement.
In addition, all partners collaborated on defining the content for the workshop to be held in Bergamo, focused on vehicle, facility, and green services procurement (Activity 3.4). 
In Activity 3.5, UPC developed the framework for evaluating the results of feasibility studies and pilot tests, which resulted in the internal publication of Deliverable D3.5.1: Instructions for Conducting Feasibility Studies and Pilot Activities.
Finally, the achievements in WP4 (supervised by CERTH) were mainly concentrated in Activities 4.1 and 4.3. These include:
-	Identifying needs and requirements: Conducted surveys, interviews, and two workshops with Public Transport Operators and Authorities (IDIADA, TUSGSAL, MOM, ATB, Theta, CARRIS, CERTH) while engaging associated parties. This process led to Internal Deliverable 4.1.1: “Joint Requirements for Strategies & Action Plans, Strategic Impact Analysis of Pilots.”
-	Developing the Communication Strategy: Drafted by Redmint and UPC, currently under review by other partners.
-	Preparing the Project Amplification Strategy: Integrated knowledge and achievements from other work packages (Redmint, Carris, UPC). A video summarizing key contributions has been published on the E-MED website as part of Deliverable 4.3.4: Promotional Videos.
All partners have initiated contact with the stakeholders at each site to develop Action Plans that will integrate E-MED solutions (Activity 4.2). Additionally, the partnership estimated the carbon footprint impact of all activities conducted during Period 1 under Activity 4.4.

The project has been making steady progress across multiple work streams, achieving key milestones in assessment, solution mapping, and infrastructure readiness. Two surveys have been successfully launched—one targeting Public Transport Operators (PTOs) and Public Transport Authorities (PTAs), and another focused on bus manufacturers—to gather critical insights for project development. These activities have been subcontracted to EIT Urban Mobility (EIT UM), ensuring expert execution and analysis.

Work continues on key deliverables, including DEL 3.5.1 (Developed frameowrk for real life testint, simulations and Feasibility studies) , DEL 4.1.1 (CERTH), and DEL 4.1.2, ensuring alignment with project objectives. Solution owners have mapped available solutions and identified essential capabilities. Additionally, infrastructure investments are in progress, with partners actively working on procurement. Offers have been requested, but delays have been reported in finalizing contracts due to supplier reservations.

Pilot activities continue to progress, with updates on their celebration and validation phases. The project consortium has been actively engaged in critical meetings and networking event such as the launch of the Green Living Areas hosted in Torino on the 10th and 11th of 2024. A Consortium Meeting was held in Bergamo in December, fostering knowledge exchange and strategic alignment.

fr.name: Increasing capacities of PTO and PTA in the EURO MED area by testing novel solutions to effectively manage public transport’s transition towards energy-efficiency and resource-efficiency
fr.description: E-MED teste et valide des solutions pour augmenter l'efficacité énergétique et l'efficacité des ressources des systèmes de transport public afin de réduire l'empreinte environnementale des transports publics et de réagir de manière flexible à l'évolution des prix de l'énergie. Cet enjeu concerne notamment les collectivités locales, les infrastructures de transport public et les fournisseurs d'énergie, mais aussi les industriels fournisseurs de véhicules électriques et les opérateurs de services. Ainsi, des solutions E-MED sont développées pour augmenter l'efficacité énergétique et la part des énergies renouvellables et pour augmenter l'efficacité des ressources et la résilience, soutenant à la planification fondée sur des preuves et à l'introduction de systèmes d'e-bus pour aider à la prise de décision objective et à la planification, à l'investissement et à l'approvisionnement intelligents dès le départ. De cette façon, les solutions E-MED prennent en charge la bonne conception d'un système de PT grâce à une organisation intelligentes des réseaux et des opérations, et un renforcement des capacités autour comme l'éco-conduite. En tant que catalyseurs clés pour réduire la consommation d'énergie et augmenter la durée de vie de la flotte et de l'infrastructure, les solutions E-MED sont axées sur les besoins des utilisateurs finaux fournissent une orientation aux acheteurs et à l'industrie. Sur la base du test et de la validation des solutions E-MED, les stratégies et les plans d'action sont optimisés dans une approche participative de co-conception. E-MED est composé de 10 partenaires allant des organisations aux autorités de transport public ainsi que des partenaires de connaissances reconnus dans le domaine des transports et de l'énergie. Pour élargir cette coopération, des partenaires associés tels que l'UITP, l'Université de Lisbonne et le CARNET sont impliqués afin de maximiser la communication et le transfert de connaissances des résultats.
fr.expected_achievements:
Provide ready-for take-up solutions to support 250 PT stakeholders with evidence-based decision-taking to improve the energy-efficiency and resilience in different electric PT subsystems: vehicle, network, energy facility.
 Provide ready-for take-up solutions to reduce the energy consumption of ebuses by means of eco-driving tools and Artificial Intelligence applied to the telemetric data.
Adapted to the needs of 500 PTA and PTO’s, develop solutions with 10 e-bus manufacturers and 10 solution providers to increase resource-efficiency of fleet, infrastructure, services, and operations..  
Develop strategies and action plans and a blueprint for coordinated, evidence-based, planning and financing, following an integrated and participatory, to enable 100 PTA and PTO improve their public transport decarbonisation strategies.
fr.actual_achievements:
In the first weeks of Period 1, the E-MED partners prepared the Steering Committee and Partnership agreements, and modified the Application Form in Jems as per JS's pre-contracting instructions. These modifications were officially approved on April 17, 2024. The kick-off meeting took place in Barcelona on February 28, 2024, with representatives from each partner.

Two additional General Assembly meetings (April 17 and June 19, 2024) were held to support project implementation, engage partners, and plan future activities.

Partners set up coordination tools for online communication and project management (Basecamp, Google Meet, M. SharePoint) and created a project website and LinkedIn channel using the JS-provided template.

Activities 1.1 and 1.2 of WP1 consisted of:

-	Conceptualizing the toolkit with integrated network-, vehicle-, and charging facility-level capabilities (all partners).
-	Defining ICT platform requirements to integrate Geographic Information Systems with modeling capabilities (CERTH, Theta).
-	Enhancing the vehicle-level planning tool by IDIADA, with AI-powered chat support for electric bus inquiries using IDIADA's data (IDIADA, CERTH, UPC).
-	Conducting a market analysis of charging-facility planning tools and identifying gaps (TUSGSAL, UPC).
-	Defining user requirements and toolkit capabilities (related to WP4).
-	Preparing content for a workshop in Thessaloniki to introduce the WP1 Planning Toolkit.

In WP2, the structure and content for the State of the Art analysis based on Eco-driving were initiated in A2.1 (supervised by UM and MOM). The specific advancements were:
-	Reviewing tools and guidelines for diesel solutions and assessing their adaptation to electric fleets, as well as Eco-driving benefits (Carris, ATB, TUSGSAL, MOM).
-	Identifying solutions for vehicles, drivers, control centers, and technology (automated, semi-automated, training).
-	Reviewing monitoring, implementation, planning, and driver reward schemes, along with regulations and requirements.
-	Developing a questionnaire for the State of the Art analysis at pilot locations (Maribor, Barcelona, Lisbon).
In Activity 2.2, UPC (Leader) and the PTOs of the E-MED project collaborated to analyze operational data from buses captured by each operator's telemetric systems. Sample datasets were obtained from TUSGSAL, Carris and ATB. The partners also undertook the following tasks:
-	Standardizing the datasets: Developed a code to translate raw data from each operator into a harmonized file format for consistency across cities.
-	Conceptualizing outputs: Defined data analytics outputs (powered by AI) in three areas: fleet analysis, driver analysis, and vehicle analysis.
-	Developing algorithms: Coded algorithms in Python for integration into the WP2 Toolkit to perform fleet analysis, focusing on headway and speed variations along routes.
The achievements in WP3 (led by TUSGSAL) aligned with the procurement methodology for fleet and infra. (A3.1) and green and circular procurement for services and operations (A3.2). The specific contributions during Period 1 were:
-	Conducting a benchmark of the current situation through interviews and surveys with PTOs, PTAs, and other partners involved in the project.
-	Defining the requirements for small to medium pilot tests, incorporating learnings and insights from past procurement processes.
-	Developing guidelines for green and circular procurement.
In addition, all partners collaborated on defining the content for the workshop to be held in Bergamo, focused on vehicle, facility, and green services procurement (Activity 3.4). 
In Activity 3.5, UPC developed the framework for evaluating the results of feasibility studies and pilot tests, which resulted in the internal publication of Deliverable D3.5.1: Instructions for Conducting Feasibility Studies and Pilot Activities.
Finally, the achievements in WP4 (supervised by CERTH) were mainly concentrated in Activities 4.1 and 4.3. These include:
-	Identifying needs and requirements: Conducted surveys, interviews, and two workshops with Public Transport Operators and Authorities (IDIADA, TUSGSAL, MOM, ATB, Theta, CARRIS, CERTH) while engaging associated parties. This process led to Internal Deliverable 4.1.1: “Joint Requirements for Strategies & Action Plans, Strategic Impact Analysis of Pilots.”
-	Developing the Communication Strategy: Drafted by Redmint and UPC, currently under review by other partners.
-	Preparing the Project Amplification Strategy: Integrated knowledge and achievements from other work packages (Redmint, Carris, UPC). A video summarizing key contributions has been published on the E-MED website as part of Deliverable 4.3.4: Promotional Videos.
All partners have initiated contact with the stakeholders at each site to develop Action Plans that will integrate E-MED solutions (Activity 4.2). Additionally, the partnership estimated the carbon footprint impact of all activities conducted during Period 1 under Activity 4.4.
Le projet progresse de manière régulière à travers plusieurs axes de travail, atteignant des étapes clés en matière d'évaluation, de cartographie des solutions et de mise à niveau des infrastructures. Deux enquêtes ont été lancées avec succès : l’une ciblant les opérateurs de transport public (PTO) et les autorités de transport public (PTA), et l’autre axée sur les fabricants de bus. Ces activités ont été sous-traitées à EIT Urban Mobility (EIT UM), garantissant une exécution et une analyse expertes.

Les travaux se poursuivent sur plusieurs livrables clés, notamment DEL 3.5.1, DEL 4.1.1 (CERTH) et DEL 4.1.2, en cohérence avec les objectifs stratégiques du projet. Les propriétaires de solutions ont cartographié les solutions disponibles et identifié les capacités essentielles. Des investissements dans les infrastructures sont en cours, les partenaires ayant demandé des offres, bien que des retards dans la finalisation des contrats aient été signalés en raison de réserves exprimées par les fournisseurs.

Les activités pilotes continuent de progresser, avec des mises à jour sur leur validation et leur mise en œuvre. Le consortium du projet a participé activement à des réunions et événements stratégiques. Une réunion du consortium a eu lieu à Bergame en décembre, facilitant l’échange de connaissances et l’alignement stratégique. Dans les prochains mois, UPC, en tant que coordinateur du projet, a été invité à un événement communautaire d’Interreg pour les partenaires espagnols le 27 mars, renforçant ainsi l’intégration du projet dans les initiatives européennes.
